 {"id":"bd-c35","title":"Track: Harness + Conformance + Parity Accounting (ffs-harness)","description":"Build the proof system: fixture generation, comparisons against Linux behavior where applicable, parity reporting, and regression gates.\n\nAcceptance: every implemented behavior has (1) a fixture or property test, (2) a harness test that exercises it end-to-end, (3) FEATURE_PARITY + harness parity numbers updated in the same change. Benchmarks are added only after fixtures exist.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:08:52.148014837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:28:15.385993623Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:28:15.385971391Z","close_reason":"All 5 sub-tasks closed: ext4 fixtures (bd-2zg), parity accounting (bd-6st), fixture workflow (bd-3q4), kernel reference capture (bd-2ij), btrfs fixtures (bd-2yi).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-2ij","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:53.179975971Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-2yi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:53.099617897Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-2zg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:53.014650837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-3q4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:52.923718680Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-6st","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:52.825671893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"bd-c35","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ffs-harness is the proof engine.

Rules:
- every new behavior needs fixtures or properties
- parity numbers must match FEATURE_PARITY.md
- only optimize after correctness vectors exist

Without this track, progress becomes vibes instead of verified parity.","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:34:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-c4vah","title":"add mounted-path btrfs hard-link refusal coverage for directory-source EPERM, non-directory parent ENOTDIR, and occupied destination EEXIST","description":"Survey finding: btrfs mounted-path FUSE E2E proves hard-link success, but not the refusal contracts that ffs-core already enforces for invalid link topologies. Scope: add mounted-path btrfs probes that assert hard-linking a directory returns EPERM, linking into a non-directory parent returns ENOTDIR, and linking onto an occupied destination returns EEXIST, with no partial mutation of link counts or directory contents. Acceptance: fuse_e2e freezes the public btrfs hard-link refusal surface for these three cases.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:58:48.235255693Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T22:35:26.031360376Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T22:35:26.028106566Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-c5t4","title":"Profile and document current FrankenFS performance hotspots","description":"Porting plan 0.8 line 89: produce an explicit hotspot profile now that correctness fixtures exist, record evidence artifact(s), and update PLAN_TO_PORT_FRANKENFS_TO_RUST.md checkbox with references.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-19T06:09:31.831199134Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T06:10:24.516179359Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T06:10:24.516157839Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","performance","porting"]}
+{"id":"bd-c88jg","title":"[FUSE] FS_IOC_FSSETXATTR ioctl — chattr -p / xfs_io fsxattr write-side parity","description":"FS_IOC_FSSETXATTR (0x401c5820) is the write-side companion to bd-6wzug (FS_IOC_FSGETXATTR). Userspace passes a 28-byte struct fsxattr; the FS sets project ID + chattr-style xflags from it. Required scope (matches Linux ext4_fileattr_set semantics): (a) fsx_projid -> Ext4Inode.projid via setattr-like path; (b) fsx_xflags -> i_flags by inverting ext4_flags_to_xflags (IMMUTABLE/APPEND/SYNC/NOATIME/NODUMP/PROJINHERIT, plus reject UNSUPPORTED bits with EOPNOTSUPP); (c) fsx_extsize/fsx_cowextsize must be 0 (ext4 does not implement them) - non-zero -> EINVAL; (d) only CAP_LINUX_IMMUTABLE can flip IMMUTABLE/APPEND - matches existing setflags ioctl gate; (e) FS_XFLAG_HASATTR is read-only (kernel-set), reject if userspace sets it. Acceptance: ioctl(fd, FS_IOC_FSSETXATTR, &in) on a regular file then FS_IOC_FSGETXATTR returns the same xflags + projid; rejects fsx_extsize != 0 with EINVAL; rejects unknown FS_XFLAG_* bits with EOPNOTSUPP.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T19:54:17.577189883Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T20:05:28.854634165Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T20:05:28.852460870Z","close_reason":"FS_IOC_FSSETXATTR shipped end-to-end. New FsOps::set_inode_fsxattr + xflags_to_ext4_flags helper + parse_fsxattr_request decoder + dispatch_ioctl branch. 8 regressions: round-trip xflags+projid, extsize/cowextsize EINVAL, HASATTR EINVAL, unknown xflag EOPNOTSUPP, clear-all-xflags drops HASATTR, parse 28B+route+commit, 27B EINVAL, read-only EROFS. Closes the chattr -p / xfs_io fsxattr parity gap with bd-6wzug.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-caex2","title":"[MOCK-FINDER] btrfs_ino_lookup only supports root objectid; non-root returns ENOENT with 'not implemented yet' comment","description":"# Mock-finder audit finding

`FsOps::btrfs_ino_lookup` at crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:16062-16082 only returns a path for `objectid == BTRFS_FIRST_FREE_OBJECTID (256)` and falls through to `Err(FfsError::Io(libc::ENOENT))` for every other inode, with the comment: `// Full back-reference walking not implemented yet.`

## Context

The prior bead bd-jfsga ('add BTRFS_IOC_INO_LOOKUP ioctl support') was closed 2026-04-21 as 'done' with acceptance criterion 'BTRFS_IOC_INO_LOOKUP returns correct path for known inodes'. The current implementation satisfies that criterion only for the single well-known root objectid; every other known inode returns ENOENT indistinguishable from 'inode does not exist'. Callers (btrfs-progs, user scripts) cannot tell the difference between 'this inode genuinely doesn't exist' and 'this file doesn't implement lookup for non-root inodes'.

## Evidence

- Backend: crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:16062-16082
- FUSE dispatcher wiring: crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:2100-2125 (correctly routes the ioctl)
- Comment verbatim: 'Full back-reference walking not implemented yet.'

## Why this is a real stub, not an intentional scope limit

Unlike write-participating MMP (crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:6531) and e2compr lzv1/lzrw3a/bzip2 (6989, 7955), which surface a bespoke `UnsupportedFeature` error with a clear scope message, this path returns a generic ENOENT that masks the limitation from callers and from conformance tools.

## Fix direction

Walk the btrfs INODE_REF / INODE_EXTREF back-references chain from the target objectid up to the tree root, assembling path components in reverse. Reference implementation: fs/btrfs/ioctl.c::btrfs_search_path_in_tree in Linux v6.19. For v1 scope, supporting only same-subvolume paths (no cross-subvolume) is acceptable if documented.

## Acceptance

- Non-root objectid that exists in the subvolume tree returns its reconstructed path.
- Non-existent objectid returns a structured NotFound / FfsError that the FUSE layer maps to ENOENT (current behavior preserved for truly-missing inodes).
- Unit + mounted-path test that resolves a file created at runtime and asserts the returned path matches.
- If cross-subvolume resolution is out of scope, emit `UnsupportedFeature('cross-subvolume btrfs_ino_lookup not implemented')` with a distinct errno mapping instead of ENOENT.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:17:45.327464791Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:49:18.168402424Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:49:18.166600714Z","close_reason":"Implemented in commit f70dcca. btrfs_ino_lookup (crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:16051) now walks INODE_REF back-references from the target objectid up to the subvolume root, assembling a /-separated path with trailing / before the terminating NUL (matches fs/btrfs/ioctl.c::btrfs_search_path_in_tree). Algorithm extracted as pure helper btrfs_build_path_from_inode_refs for unit-testability; 6 new tests cover subvolume root, single hop, multi-hop a/b/c/, missing back-ref → NotFound, cycle → Corruption, and hard-link first-wins determinism. Cross-subvolume (ROOT_REF) remains out-of-V1-scope but now returns UnsupportedFeature with a clear message rather than silent ENOENT. All 6 new tests + existing ffs-core suite pass via rch; workspace clippy green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cb0kd","title":"[HIGH] build_btrfs_readdir_image still computes invalid tree_block_csum after explicit stamping","description":"# Review finding

The current `build_btrfs_readdir_image()` fixture already writes explicit CRC32C values into both synthetic tree blocks, but `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets` still fails the six btrfs directory-walk tests with `Format("invalid field: tree_block_csum (CRC32C checksum mismatch)")`.

## Evidence
- Builder already stamps both blocks: `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23225-23378`.
- It computes `csum_root` and `csum_fs` over `image[tree + 0x20 .. tree + nodesize]` and writes them to bytes `0..4`.
- After `br-bd-o5114 repair btrfs default-root smoke fixture`, the remaining red frontier is exactly:
  - `walk_btrfs_dir_entry_items_includes_dir_index_items`
  - `btrfs_readdir_mixed_types`
  - `btrfs_readdir_empty_directory_returns_dot_dotdot`
  - `btrfs_readdir_sorted_by_index`
  - `btrfs_readdir_offset_pagination`
  - `btrfs_readdir_special_characters_in_names`
- All six still fail on the same checksum mismatch, so the root cause is no longer "missing stamp calls".

## Why this is high severity
This keeps the full workspace test gate red and shows the older broad review bead is now under-specified. The next implementation pass needs to debug the actual checksum semantics, not just add stamp writes.

## Expected fix direction
Audit the exact checksum window, node header fields, and final byte mutations for the 16 KiB synthetic readdir fixture. Verify the bytes being hashed match what the parser later validates.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:12:53Z","created_by":"cod-fs","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:36:14Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T20:36:14Z","close_reason":"Already shipped; targeted btrfs readdir repros now pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ffs-core","fixtures","qa","review","workspace-gate"]}

-{"id":"bd-elah2","title":"[MAINT] FuseInodeLocks.table grows unboundedly — every accessed inode allocates a FuseInodeLock that is never evicted","description":"# Maintainability / resource finding from modes-of-reasoning pass

## Finding

`FuseInodeLocks` at crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:703-737 stores a `Mutex<BTreeMap<InodeNumber, Arc<FuseInodeLock>>>`. Every FUSE mutation operation (create/mkdir/unlink/rmdir/rename/setattr) calls `acquire()`, which does `table.entry(ino).or_insert_with(...)` — inserting a new entry on first encounter and never evicting.

For a long-running FUSE mount that touches many inodes, the table grows monotonically. With ~80 bytes per entry (key + Arc + FuseInodeLock fields), a mount that accesses a million inodes parks ~80 MB of never-freed locks.

## Evidence

- Storage: crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:705 `table: Mutex<BTreeMap<InodeNumber, Arc<FuseInodeLock>>>`
- Insertion: line 727 `table.entry(ino).or_insert_with(|| Arc::new(FuseInodeLock::default()))`
- No `remove` call anywhere — confirmed via `rg 'table\.\(remove\|clear\|retain\)' crates/ffs-fuse/`
- No TTL, no LRU eviction, no drop-on-last-guard hook

## Why this wasn't caught earlier

Not a deadlock (inspected in /deadlock-finder pass, out of that skill's scope). Not a correctness bug. Pure resource accumulation that only matters for long-running mounts. Relevant as soon as FrankenFS targets real production workloads.

## Fix directions

1. **Drop-on-last-guard cleanup.** When `FuseInodeGuard::drop` runs, check if the `Arc<FuseInodeLock>` strong_count is 1 (we hold the last ref). If so, remove the entry from the table. Race-safe because `acquire()` holds the table mutex during insert, so we can hold it during the last-guard removal check.
2. **Epoch-based GC.** Periodically scan the table and evict entries whose `Arc` strong_count is 1 (no active guard) AND that haven't been acquired in N seconds.
3. **Cap-based eviction.** Soft cap the table size; evict idle entries LRU when the cap is exceeded.

Option 1 is cleanest and matches the natural lifecycle (entries exist only when a guard is live).

## Acceptance

- A stress test creating/unlinking 100K inodes shows FuseInodeLocks.table stays bounded (approximately proportional to concurrent guards, not to total inodes-ever-accessed).
- No regression in the sorted-ino total-order guarantee (the crate's only concurrency invariant). /deadlock-finder sweep remains CLEAN.
- Unit test: acquire+drop a single inode's guard, assert the table is empty afterward.

## Severity rationale

P3 (low) — not exploitable, not correctness, not currently a production concern (FrankenFS mounts are short-lived in V1.x). But it is a real resource leak that will matter when V2+ targets longer-running deployments. Worth tracking so it doesn't get lost.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:28:54.401068265Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T22:09:57.356312229Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Worth tracking so it doesn't get lost.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:28:54.401068265Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T19:53:50.228801103Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T19:53:50.226914009Z","close_reason":"Already shipped in commit 3b07940 (FuseInodeLocks::table eviction on last guard drop, 4 unit tests).  {"id":"bd-eptw","title":"Repo hygiene: ignore local benchmark scratch artifacts","description":"Context: bv triage reports all existing beads closed (no actionable work). Current workspace repeatedly accumulates untracked local-only benchmark artifacts (loopback ext4 images, mount staging dirs, local sqlite backups) that create noise and accidental-commit risk for multi-agent work.\n\nScope:\n- Add targeted .gitignore rules for local benchmark scratch artifacts only.\n- Do not ignore tracked benchmark baselines or fixture assets.\n- Validate rules via git check-ignore on representative paths.\n\nAcceptance:\n- Existing untracked scratch paths are ignored by git.\n- Tracked baseline files remain tracked/unaffected.\n- Agent-mail update posted with bead id and completion summary.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-19T01:53:17.689900464Z","created_by":"SilverLake","updated_at":"2026-02-19T01:54:01.309714130Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T01:54:01.309693260Z","close_reason":"Completed: scratch artifact ignore rules added and validated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent-coordination","benchmark","ci","hygiene"],"comments":[{"id":265,"issue_id":"bd-eptw","author":"SilverLake","text":"Implemented targeted .gitignore rules for benchmark scratch artifacts and local sqlite backups; validated with git check-ignore for mount_probe.ext4, mount_recovery_probe.ext4, mount_probe_mounts/, ffs_cli_inspect_probe.stderr, and storage.sqlite3.bak. Confirmed tracked baseline file baselines/hyperfine/20260218/ffs_cli_parity.json is not ignored.","created_at":"2026-02-19T01:54:01Z"}]}

Scope: add read-only btrfs probes for unlink, rmdir, and rename that assert exact EROFS and unchanged directory entries. Acceptance: btrfs FUSE E2E freezes read-only operator-visible refusal for unlink, rmdir, and rename.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:27:15.710372657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T19:57:48.915853489Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T19:57:48.915853489Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-qorr","title":"Add exact golden coverage for ext4 feature-diagnostics display contract","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T21:00:36.531313918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T21:05:19.860920754Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T21:05:19.858805611Z","close_reason":"Added exact golden coverage for ext4 feature-diagnostics display strings, including a synthetic all-fields-populated contract and a superblock-derived contract covering missing-required and unknown-bit formatting; validated with rch cargo test -p ffs-ext4 feature_diagnostics -- --nocapture, rch cargo check -p ffs-ext4 --all-targets, rch cargo clippy -p ffs-ext4 --all-targets -- -D warnings, and rch cargo fmt --check.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-qpoys","title":"[FUSE] splice/sendfile pipe-to-file fast path","description":"Implement zero-copy fast path for splice(2) and sendfile(2) when reading from a pipe and writing to a regular file (or vice-versa) over the FUSE mount.

Context:
- Linux splice/sendfile expect the page cache or pipe-buf to be moved without a userspace bounce. Standard FUSE userspace can't actually bypass userspace, but it CAN advertise FUSE_PASSTHROUGH or use FUSE_NO_OPEN_SUPPORT + dispatcher hints to short-circuit large copies.
- For ffs-fuse, plumbing should: detect splice/sendfile ioctl path (from request flags or FUSE_WRITE / FUSE_READ with the splice flag set), batch the I/O into block-aligned segments, route through MVCC-aware extent mapping without per-byte copy.
- Reference: fs/splice.c, fs/read_write.c::do_sendfile, FUSE FOPEN_DIRECT_IO + FOPEN_NO_FLUSH semantics.

Acceptance:
- A test that writes 16 MiB through splice() (pipe -> file in mounted FUSE) and verifies the data + ensures no per-page memcpy in the hot path that's bigger than O(extents).
- xfstests generic/{255, 263, 462} (splice/sendfile-touching) pass under the FUSE mount with --rw.
- Bench delta: at least 2x throughput vs. the naive read+write loop on a 1 GiB file.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T05:24:10.085436820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T05:24:10.085436820Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-qpoys","title":"[FUSE] splice/sendfile pipe-to-file fast path","description":"Implement zero-copy fast path for splice(2) and sendfile(2) when reading from a pipe and writing to a regular file (or vice-versa) over the FUSE mount.

Context:
- Linux splice/sendfile expect the page cache or pipe-buf to be moved without a userspace bounce. Standard FUSE userspace can't actually bypass userspace, but it CAN advertise FUSE_PASSTHROUGH or use FUSE_NO_OPEN_SUPPORT + dispatcher hints to short-circuit large copies.
- For ffs-fuse, plumbing should: detect splice/sendfile ioctl path (from request flags or FUSE_WRITE / FUSE_READ with the splice flag set), batch the I/O into block-aligned segments, route through MVCC-aware extent mapping without per-byte copy.
- Reference: fs/splice.c, fs/read_write.c::do_sendfile, FUSE FOPEN_DIRECT_IO + FOPEN_NO_FLUSH semantics.

Acceptance:
- A test that writes 16 MiB through splice() (pipe -> file in mounted FUSE) and verifies the data + ensures no per-page memcpy in the hot path that's bigger than O(extents).
- xfstests generic/{255, 263, 462} (splice/sendfile-touching) pass under the FUSE mount with --rw.
- Bench delta: at least 2x throughput vs. the naive read+write loop on a 1 GiB file.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T05:24:10.085436820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T19:28:52.952294083Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-qpt5","title":"Add SIMD-accelerated checksum computation","description":"# Add SIMD-accelerated checksum computation

## GOAL
Accelerate CRC32C and BLAKE3 checksum computation using SIMD instructions.

## BACKGROUND (Extreme Software Optimization)
Checksum computation is on the hot path:
- Every block read/write verifies checksum
- ARC cache hits still verify (trust but verify)
- RaptorQ symbols have checksums

SIMD can provide 4-16x speedup depending on instruction set.

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. CRC32C Acceleration
```rust
pub fn crc32c_simd(data: &[u8], seed: u32) -> u32 {
    #[cfg(target_arch = "x86_64")]
    {
        if is_x86_feature_detected!("sse4.2") {
            return crc32c_sse42(data, seed);
        }
    }
    #[cfg(target_arch = "aarch64")]
    {
        if is_aarch64_feature_detected!("crc") {
            return crc32c_arm_crc(data, seed);
        }
    }
    crc32c_fallback(data, seed)
}
```

### 2. BLAKE3 (Already SIMD)
- blake3 crate already uses SIMD
- Ensure proper feature flags enabled
- Benchmark to confirm

### 3. Batch Checksum
```rust
/// Compute checksums for multiple blocks in parallel
pub fn batch_checksum(blocks: &[&[u8]], algo: ChecksumAlgo) -> Vec<u32> {
    // Use rayon for multi-block parallelism
    // Use SIMD within each block
}
```

### 4. Feature Detection at Runtime
- Detect CPU features once at startup
- Store in global (or thread-local)
- Dispatch to appropriate implementation

## TESTS
1. Unit: SIMD matches fallback results
2. Unit: All supported CPUs tested (via CI matrix)
3. Benchmark: Before/after comparison
4. Property: Random data, SIMD == fallback

## LOGGING
- Feature detection (info): detected SIMD capabilities
- Batch checksum (trace): block count, duration

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Correctness: SIMD == fallback for all inputs
2. Performance: >2x speedup on supported CPUs
3. Graceful fallback on unsupported CPUs
4. No unsafe code (use safe SIMD wrappers)

## NOTES
Use existing crates:
- crc32c crate has SIMD support
- blake3 crate is already optimized
Main work is ensuring proper feature flags and benchmarking.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:55:14.096946312Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T15:05:05.163955333Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T15:05:05.163936237Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["optimization","perf","simd"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:57:00.533583752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:29.930946133Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":207,"issue_id":"bd-qpt5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: general
Mapped graveyard sections: 0.8 Runtime Decision Core + 0.19 Evidence Ledger
EV score: 9 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: B
Budgeted mode: controller_tick_ms=1000, policy_timeout_ms=200
Fallback trigger: static config profile

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-qqowd","title":"add mounted-path btrfs read-only EROFS coverage for fsync fdatasync and fsyncdir","description":"Survey finding: btrfs fuse_e2e currently proves fsync, fdatasync, and fsyncdir success on writable mounts, but the mounted-path read-only rejection contract is not frozen even though ffs-fuse and ffs-core already return EROFS for these sync operations on read-only mounts. Scope: add btrfs read-only mounted-path probes for file sync_all, sync_data, and directory sync_all that assert exact EROFS and unchanged data/dirent state. Acceptance: btrfs FUSE E2E freezes operator-visible read-only sync refusal for fsync, fdatasync, and fsyncdir.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:20:27.269990402Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:32:36.746576644Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:32:36.744719148Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-qrqdk","title":"[REVIEW][MEDIUM] Jbd2Writer emits unchecksummed descriptor/revoke/commit blocks — replay with verify_checksums=true rejects them on checksummed journals","description":"# Review finding (review-only pass on 2026-04-23)

## Location

`crates/ffs-journal/src/lib.rs` `Jbd2Writer::commit_transaction` (lines ~982-1180) and the `Jbd2Writer` struct (~841-851).

## Finding

The JBD2-compatible writer produces three kinds of blocks — descriptor, revoke, and commit — and does not stamp a checksum into any of them:

- Descriptor blocks (L1056-L1095): built with `vec![0; bs]` + header + tags, written verbatim. No tail-checksum at `block[bs-4..bs]`.
- Revoke blocks (L1126-L1153): built with header + entries, written verbatim. No tail-checksum.
- Commit blocks (L1160-L1164): built with just `encode_jbd2_header`; `h_chksum_type`, `h_chksum_size`, and `h_chksum[0]` at offset 16 are all zero bytes. No CRC written.

The replay-side `verify_jbd2_block_checksum` (L282-L321) checks all three types when `sb.has_checksum()` is true:

- Descriptor/revoke: reads `stored = read_be_u32(block, bs-4)`, computes via `checksum_jbd2_tail_zeroed_block(block, seed)`, compares.
- Commit: reads `stored = read_be_u32(block, JBD2_COMMIT_CHKSUM_OFFSET)` (offset 16), computes `!crc32c_append(!seed, &temp)` with the 4 bytes at 16 zeroed, compares.

For writer-produced blocks, `stored == 0` in every case but `computed` is a non-zero CRC of the block bytes, so the comparison returns false and `replay_jbd2_inner` breaks out of the scan (L523).

## Impact

A writer-produced transaction on a checksummed journal (any of `JBD2_FEATURE_COMPAT_CHECKSUM`, `JBD2_FEATURE_INCOMPAT_CSUM_V2`, `JBD2_FEATURE_INCOMPAT_CSUM_V3`, or `JBD2_FEATURE_INCOMPAT_ASYNC_COMMIT`) is unreplayable with the default `ReplayOptions { verify_checksums: true }`. Modern `mkfs.ext4` enables `metadata_csum` by default, which sets `JBD2_FEATURE_INCOMPAT_CSUM_V3` on the journal superblock, so any real-world ext4 image with default features falls into this path.

## Why this is currently latent (but still a bug)

`Jbd2Writer::attach_jbd2_writer` is not called from any production binary today (grep across `crates/ffs-fuse`, `crates/ffs-cli`, `crates/ffs-harness`, `crates/ffs` finds no callers); only tests construct writers. Tests avoid triggering the bug by either running without a JBD2 superblock in the journal, or by post-stamping checksums via the `stamp_commit_checksum` / `stamp_descriptor_or_revoke_checksum` helpers (lines 2591-2608), which exist precisely because the writer does not.

When anyone wires `attach_jbd2_writer` into the `ffs mount --rw` / `commit_transaction_journaled` path (see `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:4312`), a checksummed ext4 image will fail to replay on the next mount, silently losing the transaction's writes.

## Suggested fix

1. Add `has_checksum: bool` and `csum_seed: u32` fields to `Jbd2Writer` alongside `is_64bit`.
2. `Jbd2Writer::open` already reads the journal superblock (L889) — have it also populate these fields via `sb.has_checksum()` and `sb.csum_seed()`.
3. Provide a `Jbd2Writer::with_checksum_config(region, start_seq, has_checksum, csum_seed)` constructor for the `Jbd2Writer::new` callers that need it.
4. In `commit_transaction`, when `has_checksum` is true:
   - After filling a descriptor block but before `dev.write_block`, stamp the tail via `checksum_jbd2_tail_zeroed_block(&desc, self.csum_seed).unwrap()` and write into the last 4 bytes.
   - Same for revoke blocks.
   - For commit blocks, zero `h_chksum[0]` (offset 16), compute `!crc32c::crc32c_append(!seed, &commit)`, write into offset 16.
5. Fix `max_tags_per_descriptor` / `max_revoke_entries` / `blocks_needed` to reserve 4 tail bytes when `has_checksum` is true.

## Acceptance

- New regression test: build a `MemBlockDevice`, stamp a valid JBD2 superblock with `feature_incompat = JBD2_FEATURE_INCOMPAT_CSUM_V3` + a non-zero UUID at region block 0, call `Jbd2Writer::open` (or `with_checksum_config`), commit one transaction with a single write, then call `replay_jbd2_with_options(cx, dev, region, ReplayOptions { verify_checksums: true })` and assert `outcome.stats.replayed_blocks == 1` and the target block was overwritten with the payload.
- Existing non-checksummed tests keep passing.
- `commit_transaction_journaled` in `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs` — still has no production caller, but when it does, the journal round-trips through a verify_checksums=true replay.

## Severity

[MEDIUM] — real data-loss bug with a clear trigger (metadata_csum ext4 + Jbd2Writer write path), but unreachable from any shipping binary today. Worth fixing before anyone wires the writer into the mount --rw path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:52:19.015585Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:58:44.436959096Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:58:44.435067924Z","close_reason":"Jbd2Writer stamps descriptor, revoke, and commit checksums for verified replay","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
